Ms. Linda Cassidy
PoIitical Reform Consultant
Techni-cal Assi-stance & Analysis Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 rrJrr Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 9581-4
RE: CONFIRIi'.ATION OF TEIJEPIIONE ADVICE REGARDING
TRANSUITTAL OF LATE CONTRIBUTION/I,ATE INDEPENDENT
EXPENDITURE REPORTS
Dear Ms. Cassidy:
Pursuant to our recent telephone conversations,
this letter seeks written confirmation (formal written
advice) regarding the appropriate methods for
transmission of Late Contribution Reports and Late
Independent Expenditure Reports. This advice is sought
on behalf of Lance H. O1son, who is treasurer for
numerous committees that are subject to these reporting
reguirements.
I posed two guestions to you. I am seeking
wri-tten confirmation of your responses because neither
you nor I could locate specific prior written advice
from the Commission which confirms the advice you qave
me. In the future, the response to this l-etter should
do that.
Question 1: When a concributj-on is received by a
committee on day one and the contrj-bution is subject to
l-ate contribution reporting, is it perrnissible to
deposit the Late Contribution Report 1n the guaranteed
overnight delivery pickup within 24 hours of receipt
(i.e., on day two)? This question arose because some
clients of ours had received conflicting advice
indicating that the Late Contribution Report had to be
filed (arrive at the filing officer's location) within
24 hours of receipt.
Answer 1: The Late Contribution Report complies
with the requirements of Section 84203 (b) if it is
deposited with an appropriate guaranteed overnight


us. Linda cassidy
June 1, 1992
Paqe 2
delivery service within 24 hours of receipt (i.e., the next
calendar day) .
The same is true of a Late Independent Expenditure Report.
It meets the requirements of section a42o4(a) if it is deposited
with an appropriate guaranteed overnight delivery service within
24 hours of the expendJ,ture being rnade.
For makers of late contributions, the deposj,t with an
appropriate guaranteed overnight delivery service must occur
within 24 hours of when the contribution was trmadetr as that term
is defined in Regulation 18421.1(b).
Question 2: For purposes of Late Contribution Reports and
Late Independent Expenditure Reports under Sections 84203 and
84204, does an appropriate guaranteed overnight delivery service
include comrnercial services which provide guaranteed overnight
delivery (such as Federal Express, UPS, DI{L, Airborne, etc.)? 
Or
is it limited to rrguaranteed overnight mail through the United
States Postal Servicett?
Ansrrer 2: 
Based upon the Baqatelos Advice Letter,
No. A-86-305, you advised that any conmercial guaranteed
overnight delivery service may be utilized; it is not lirnited to
just the United States Postal Service. Ef fectj-ve with 1987, the
Political Reform Act has provided that the Supplemental Pre-
Election Statements nay be fited using trguaranteed overnight
delivery service." 
(Section 84200.7 (a) (2) and (b) (2); Section
8400.8(b) and (c) .) 
The Conmission staff has concLuded that this
more inclusive provision best serves the purposes of the Act and
is interpreting the provisions of Sections 84203 and 84204 to
allow for transmission of these reporEs by any rguaranteed
overnight delivery servicerr which includes services other than
the United States postal Service.
Thank you for your attention to this matter. If you have
any questions, please do not hesitate to caII rne.
Very truly yours,
EONG & LEIDIGH
